chapter_title.en: Chapter 18 – Testimony of the slanderer and the penalised

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/253)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (4/253)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمَحْدُودِ إِنْ تَابَ تُقْبَلُ شَهَادَتُهُ فَقَالَ إِذَا تَابَ وَ تَوْبَتُهُ أَنْ يَرْجِعَ مِمَّا قَالَ وَ يُكْذِبَ نَفْسَهُ عِنْدَ الْإِمَامِ وَ عِنْدَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ فَإِذَا فَعَلَ فَإِنَّ عَلَى الْإِمَامِ أَنْ يَقْبَلَ شَهَادَتَهُ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhamad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Ibn Sinan who said,

‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the penalised one (by the Hadd), if he were to repent, would his testimony be accepted?’ So heasws said: ‘When he repents, and his repentance is that he should retract from what he had said, and belie himself in the presence of the Imamasws, and in the presence of the Muslims. So when he does it, so it would be upon the Imamasws that he accept his testimony after that’.
en.sarwar: 6. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn Mahbub from ibn Sinan who has narrated the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about the case of a man who is subjected to penalty; then he repents: if his testimony becomes acceptable. He (the Imam) said, ‘If he repents which is retracting what he has said and calls himself a liar before the Imam of the Muslims. When he does this then it is necessary for the Imam to accept his testimony thereafter .
